title: Add a race-lap ranking auxiliary model grouped by race context
summary: Pit calls are partly relative decisions: on a given lap, only a few cars are plausible stoppers.
rationale: Pit calls are partly relative decisions: on a given lap, only a few cars are plausible stoppers. Learning-to-rank is built for ordering candidates within a query group, and XGBoost/LightGBM support LambdaMART-style objectives over grouped samples.
implementation_hint: Create query groups such as `Race_Year x LapNumber` or `Race_Year x LapNumber x compound_regime`; train a ranking model (`rank:ndcg`, `rank:pairwise`, or `lambdarank`) to score which drivers are most likely to pit next lap. Blend its OOF predictions with the binary classifier, especially in dry races where tactical ordering matters most.
expected_effect: Should improve relative ordering within each lap and recover signal missed by independent row-wise classification.
risk: Some groups may have very few positives or unstable size; ranking loss may help AUC only after careful blending, not as a standalone model.
